Google 


This  is  a  digital  copy  of  a  book  that  was  preserved  for  generations  on  library  shelves  before  it  was  carefully  scanned  by  Google  as  part  of  a  project 

to  make  the  world's  books  discoverable  online. 

It  has  survived  long  enough  for  the  copyright  to  expire  and  the  book  to  enter  the  public  domain.  A  public  domain  book  is  one  that  was  never  subject 

to  copyright  or  whose  legal  copyright  term  has  expired.  Whether  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  may  vary  country  to  country.  Public  domain  books 

are  our  gateways  to  the  past,  representing  a  wealth  of  history,  culture  and  knowledge  that's  often  difficult  to  discover. 

Marks,  notations  and  other  maiginalia  present  in  the  original  volume  will  appear  in  this  file  -  a  reminder  of  this  book's  long  journey  from  the 

publisher  to  a  library  and  finally  to  you. 

Usage  guidelines 

Google  is  proud  to  partner  with  libraries  to  digitize  public  domain  materials  and  make  them  widely  accessible.  Public  domain  books  belong  to  the 
public  and  we  are  merely  their  custodians.  Nevertheless,  this  work  is  expensive,  so  in  order  to  keep  providing  tliis  resource,  we  liave  taken  steps  to 
prevent  abuse  by  commercial  parties,  including  placing  technical  restrictions  on  automated  querying. 
We  also  ask  that  you: 

+  Make  non-commercial  use  of  the  files  We  designed  Google  Book  Search  for  use  by  individuals,  and  we  request  that  you  use  these  files  for 
personal,  non-commercial  purposes. 

+  Refrain  fivm  automated  querying  Do  not  send  automated  queries  of  any  sort  to  Google's  system:  If  you  are  conducting  research  on  machine 
translation,  optical  character  recognition  or  other  areas  where  access  to  a  large  amount  of  text  is  helpful,  please  contact  us.  We  encourage  the 
use  of  public  domain  materials  for  these  purposes  and  may  be  able  to  help. 

+  Maintain  attributionTht  GoogXt  "watermark"  you  see  on  each  file  is  essential  for  in  forming  people  about  this  project  and  helping  them  find 
additional  materials  through  Google  Book  Search.  Please  do  not  remove  it. 

+  Keep  it  legal  Whatever  your  use,  remember  that  you  are  responsible  for  ensuring  that  what  you  are  doing  is  legal.  Do  not  assume  that  just 
because  we  believe  a  book  is  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  the  United  States,  that  the  work  is  also  in  the  public  domain  for  users  in  other 
countries.  Whether  a  book  is  still  in  copyright  varies  from  country  to  country,  and  we  can't  offer  guidance  on  whether  any  specific  use  of 
any  specific  book  is  allowed.  Please  do  not  assume  that  a  book's  appearance  in  Google  Book  Search  means  it  can  be  used  in  any  manner 
anywhere  in  the  world.  Copyright  infringement  liabili^  can  be  quite  severe. 

About  Google  Book  Search 

Google's  mission  is  to  organize  the  world's  information  and  to  make  it  universally  accessible  and  useful.   Google  Book  Search  helps  readers 
discover  the  world's  books  while  helping  authors  and  publishers  reach  new  audiences.  You  can  search  through  the  full  text  of  this  book  on  the  web

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalc^^ue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
booksy  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  hnmediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-39416,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    GFboup  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dbamatio  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  ''£,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

LIBRARY  OF  CONGRESS 

COPYRIGHT  OFFICE 


CATALOGUE 


OF 


COPYRIGHT  ENTRIES 


PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONQRESS 
OF  MARCH  8,  1891,  AND  OF  JUNE  80,  1906 


Part  1: 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 
MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


New  Series,  Volume  4 

No.  2 

January  9, 1908 


WASHINGTON 

government  printing  office 
Library  Division 

1908 


BOOKS ;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includifig  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905^  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
|)hlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-40681,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboup  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dbamatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,''  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

LIBRARY  OF  CONGRESS 

COPYRIGHT  OFFICE 


CATALOGUE 


OF 


COPYRIGHT  ENTRIES 


FUBUSHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONGRESS 
OF  MARCH  8,  1891,  AND  OF  JUNE  80,  1906 


Part  1: 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 
MAPS  AND  CHARTS  . 


New  Series,  Volume  4 

No.  3 

January  16,  1908 


WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS ;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  Mardi  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-41813,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboup  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  ''£,''  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets. 


403-574 


LIBRARY  OF  CONGRESS 

COPYRIGHT  OFFICE 


CATALOGUE 


OF 


COPYRIGHT  ENTRIES 


PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONGRESS 
OF  MARCH  8,  1891,  AND  OF  JUNE  80,  1906 


Part  1: 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 
MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


New  Series,  Volume  4 

No.  4 

January  23, 1908 


WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS ;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles^ of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyrig^ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  7-3S246,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  ptiated  card  for  this  title. 

Qbottp  1

Books.    Group  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including^  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,**  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

[nclndes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
fir^  group  contahis  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
*'  book,"  including  preliminary  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  socie- 
ties, and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  i)eriodical8.  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printed  in  the  last  number  of  the 
Gatalooux  of  each  month.  When  the  author  is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  given  book,  an  asterisk  appears  after  the  author's  name ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  ot 
entry.

CLASS  E.  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


Arranged  alpliabetlcally  by  the  sabject  word  of  each  title.    For  index  of  proprietors,  see 

end  of  Catalogue. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last 
inue  of  the  catalogue  of  each  month. 

To  sare  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated.  Thus  the  notice 
after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copyright  by  G.  S.  Chapln,  Chicago, 
111.    Class  E,  XXc,  no.  12178,  12179,  Dec.  23,  1007 ;   2  copies  received  Dec.  ^,  1907."

PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONGRESS 
OF  MARCH  8,  1891,  AND  OF  JUNE  SO,  1906 


Part  1: 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE

BOOKS ;  DEAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arrange  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includnig  the 
titles  of  ^  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  19057  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  ''book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catai,ogu«  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  c.  g.,  8-981,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

umes)           853

tions to  periodicals,  etc.)  completed  by  deposit 1,654

included  in  Catalogue  since  Jan.  1,  1908 89 

Total  number  of  entries  of  books  completed  by  deposit 2,596 


Books.     Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  2.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

COPYRIGHT  OFFICE 


CATALOGUE 


OF 


COPYRIGHT  ENTRIES 


PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OP  THE  ACTS  OP  CONGRESS 
OP  If  ARCH  a,  1891,  AND  OP  JUNE  80,  1906 


Part  1: 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS

New  Series,  Volume  4 

No.  7 

February  13, 1908 


WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
I>hlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogus  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-3281,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  tlie  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Geoup  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dbamatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  '*£,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS ;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,''  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri^^ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  n  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  &-3520,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  nnmber  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

tions to  periodicals,  etc.,  completed  by  deposit 1,654

Total  number  of  entries  of  books  completed  by  deposit —      2,826 

Books.     Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  11.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  eadi 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  adphabets.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  In  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
Issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1005,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  to  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
"  book,"  including  preliminary  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  socie- 
ties, and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  Included  in  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  In  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

BOOKS.     GROUP  2. 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  prhited  In  the  last  number  of  the 
Catalogue  of  each  month.  When  the  author  Is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  given  book,  an  asterisk  appears  after  the  author's  name ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.

Number  of  entries  of  Dramatic  compositions  completed  by  deposit  of  2 
copies  each,  included  in  the  Catalogue  since  Jan.  1,  1008 322 


CLASS  E,  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


Arranged  alphabetically  by  the  subject  word  of  each  title.    For  index  of  proprietors,  see 

end  of  Catalooub. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumnlated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last 
Me  of  the  Catalogue  of  each  month.  To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each 
title  is  abbreviated.  Every  entry  in  the  Catalogue  implies  the  deposit  of  copies,  the 
title  of  no  map  being  included  in  the  Catalogue  unless  copies  are  on  nle,  but  if  the  conies 
have  been  received  on  the  same  date  as  the  day  of  entry  the  statement  of  the  receipt  of 
copies  on  that  date  is  not  repeated  but  is  understood. 

The  notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copyrighted  by  San- 
bom  map  company,  New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  E.  XXc,  no.  12380,  Jan.  21,  1908;  2  copies 
received  to  complete  copyright  Jan.  21,  1908."

PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONORESS 
OF  MARCH  fl,  1891,  AMD  OF  JUNE  30,  U06

New  Series,  Volume  4 

No.  10 

March  5, 1908 


WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includiiig  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  19057  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,''  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  CATAi«OGue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  th*  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-^3960,  is  the  Library  of  Congreat 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Summary  Statement  of  Deposits  Received  and  Crbditbd, 
Included  in  the  Catalogue  since  Jan.  1,  1908. 

Number  of  entries  of  books.  Group  I,  completed  by  deposits  (vol- 
umes)        1,239 

Number  of  entries  of  books,  Group  II  (pamphlets,  leaflets,  contribu- 
tions to  periodicals,  etc.)  completed  by  deposit 3,133 

Num!)er  of  ad  interim  copyright  entries,  under  act  of  Mar.  3,  1905, 
included  in  the  Catalogue  since  Jan.  1,  1908 184 

Total  number  of  entries  of  books  completed  by  deposit 4,556 


Books.    Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

CiiASS  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  ^irrted 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includinjg^  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  19057  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  8ie  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  ntunber  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  ateve  iht  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-5581,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  munber  lor  the  printed  card  for  tbit  title.

BOOKS,   GBOUP  I  411 

no.  J  i^  Mar.  la,  igo8 

Books.    Gboxtp  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
dphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Ckabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "£,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

COPYRIGHT  ENTRIES 


PUBLISHED  BY  AUTHORITY  OF  THE  ACTS  OF  CONGRESS

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphletSi  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905^  for 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,*'  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catai/x;ue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  eadi  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-6967,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Grout  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  *'E "  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  If  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  bool^s  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
Issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
"  book,"  including  preliminary  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  socie- 
ties, and  directories,  hei^books,  etc.  The  titles  Included  in  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printed  in  the  last  number  of  the 
Catalogue  of  each  month.  When  the  author  is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  giyen  book,  an  asterisk  appears  after  the  author's  name ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giying  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.

CLASS  D,  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 

The  statement  as  to  receipt  of  2  copies  is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  have  heen 
receWed  on  the  day  of  entry. 

Arraniced  alphahetically  by  titles.  For  index  of  proprietors  and  authors  see  end  of 
Catalooub. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  The  titles  of  Dramatic 
Compositions  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  in  the  last  issue  of  the  Cata- 
T.OOUB  of  each  month ;  thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title  if  printed  In  full  would  read 
*'  Copyrighted  by  Marie  Bankhead  Owen,  Montgomery,  ^Ua.  Class  D,  XXc,  no.  12460, 
Feb.  28.  1008 ;  2  copies  received  Feb.  28,  1908."

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphletSi  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includinjg  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  190Si  foi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-8310,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.     Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained h\  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  **R,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

Clas&  a,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets^  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  .  The  first  group,  contains  the^  titles  .of  books  for  .which  printed 
catalogue  curds  ari  issued  by  the  Librai-y  of  Congress,  etc.,  includmg  the 
titles  of  foreign 'books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  fo| 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second-  group  contains  the  titles,  pf  pa^nv 
p^letSj  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri^^ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  ntimber  at^the  end  of  ar  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-9499,  is  the  Library  of  Cangress 

order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  tliis  title.

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
mcliiding  reports  of  court  decisions,  dfrcctories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  arc  con- 
tained in. Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a. number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  1  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


*.  -It. 


•     ■  • 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 
LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHAETS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  arc  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905?  foi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preltminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-10636,  is  the  library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    (}boup  n 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  Ihe  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabtb 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

i 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  foi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
bcK)ks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e,  g.,  8-11090,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E/*  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issued  by  the  Library  of  Ck>ngress,  etc..  Including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1906,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  to  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
**  book,"  Including  preliminary  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  socie- 
ties, and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

BOOKS.     GROUP  2. 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printed  in  the  last  number  of  the 
Catalogue  of  each  month.  When  the  author  is  also  the  copyright  proprietor 
of  a  giyen  book,  an  asterisk  appears  after  the  author's  name ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  is  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.

CLASS  E.  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


Arranged  alphabetically  by  the  subject  word  of  each  title.    For  Index  of  proprietors,  see 

end  of  Catalogue. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last 
Issue  of  the  catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  entries  followed  by  a  *  have  been .  delayed  Inclusion  in  the  Catalogue  on  account 
of  necessary  correspondence,  etc. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  Is  abbreviated.  Thus  the  notice 
after  the  first  title.  If  printed  In  full,  would  read  :  "  Copyright  by  Sanborn  map  co.,  New 
York,  N.  Y.,  Class  E,  JfiCc,  no.  12781,  Apr.  1,  1908 ;  2  copies  received  Apr.  1,  1908."

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  IQOST  foi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
I>hlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catai«ogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-12208,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboup  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Grou^  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Chabts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  *H*'  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

IQ08 


BOOKS ;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includmg  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  foi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyrig^ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.  8-13371,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Gboup  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
P«irt  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

OF

WASHINGTON 

GOVERNMENT  PRINTING  OFFICE 

LIBRARY  DIVISION 

1908 


BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND 

CHABTS 


Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc,  includinjgr  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  IQOST  f oi 
ad  interim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  ''book,"  including  preliminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  uroceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyrif^ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-14696,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Geoup  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 

Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS ;  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


CLASS  A.  BOOKS. 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  In  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  If  anonjrmous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
Issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
**  book,"  including  preliminary  reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  socie- 
ties, and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

CLASS  D.  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 


The  statement  as  to  receipt  of  2  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  hare  been 
Tecelved  on  the  day  of  entry. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  titles.  For  Index  of  proprietors  and  authors  see  end  of 
Catalogub. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreyiated.  The  titles  of  Dramatie 
Compositions  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last  issue  of  the  Cata- 
LOODB  of  each  month ;  thus  the  notice  after  the  first  title  if  prfhted  in  full  would  read 
"  Copyrighted  by  Edgar  Bamum,  New  York,  N.  T.  Class  D,  XX c,  no.  12788,  Apr.  20, 
1908 ;  1  copy  Apr.  20,  1908 ;  1  copy  May  1,  1908."

CLASS  E.  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


Arranged  alphabetically  bj  the  subject  word  of  each  title.     For  index  of  proprietors,  see 

end  of  Catalogue. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  In  the  last 
Issue  of  the  catalogue  of  each  month. 

To  save  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated.  Thus  the  notice 
after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read :  "  Copyrighted  by  Sanborn  map  com- 
pany. New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  E,  XXc,  no.  12959,  May  4,  1908 ;  2  copies  received  to  com- 
plete copyright  May  4,  1908."

Clasb  a,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  authof  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
tkle.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.>  including  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  IQOST  for 
ad  iterim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals^  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  iftcluding  preUminary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  grotip  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  CATAtocuE  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyri|;ht,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  mdex  to 
this  title. 

The  nmnber  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen^  e.  g.,  8-15776,  is  the  Libraxy  tfl  CfQgiess 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts. 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND

Class  A,  Books 

Includes  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or,  if  anonymous,  by 
title.  The  first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  includuj^  the 
titles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  19^  for 
ad  itcrim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  "book,"  including  prelimtnary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  the  second  group  will  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Cataw)guE  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  the  index  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-16493,  is  the  Library  of  Congress 
order  number  for  tko  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.    Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avdid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts. 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

Includes  )>ooks,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in 
two  groups,  arranged  alphabetically  t>y  author  or,  if  ^jiqpymou^;  by 
title.  The  First  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which*  printed 
catalogue  cards  are  issued  b^  the  Library  of  Congress,  etf.,  includaa^  the 
tiiles  of  foreign  books  registered  under  the  ^t  of  March  %^  19D5)  fpr 
ad  iterim  protection.  The  second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pam- 
phlets, leaflets,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  all  other  produc- 
tions registered  under  the  legal  designation  ^'i^og^c,"  including  ^elimifiary 
reports  of  court  decisions,  proceedings  of  societies,  and  directories,  herd- 
books,  etc.  The  titles  included  in  die  second  group  wil)  be  printed  in 
the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a 
citation  number  by  means  of  which  reference  is  made  in  Hie  indiex  to 
this  title. 

The  number  at  the  end  of  a  title  and  immediately  above  the  copyright 
notice,  printed  with  a  hyphen,  e.  g.,  8-17563,  is  t£ie  Li)>rAxy  ^f  Cp^fffeaa 
order  number  for  the  printed  card  for  this  title.

Books.     Group  II 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  and  serial  publications, 
including  reports  of  court  decisions,  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.,  are  con- 
tained in  Group  II.  To  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small 
alphabets,  the  titles  in  this  group  are  printed  in  the  last  weekljf  issue  of 
Part  I  for  each  month,  thus  reducing  the  alphabetical  indexes  to  12  for 
each  year. 


Dramatic  Compositions,  and  Maps  and  Charts. 

The  titles  for  entries  made  in  Class  "D,"  Dramatic  Compositions,  and 
Class  "E,"  Maps  and  Charts,  are  printed  in  the  last  weekly  issue  for  each 
month  to  avoid  the  inconvenience  of  a  number  of  small  alphabets.

BOOKS;  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS;  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 


CLASS  A,  BOOKS. 

Inchides  books,  pamphlets,  leaflets,  etc.,  and  contributions  to  periodicals,  in  two 
groups,  arranged  alphabetically  by  author  or.  If  anonymous,  by  title.  The 
first  group  contains  the  titles  of  books  for  which  printed  catalogue  cards  are 
issued  by  the  Library  of  Congress,  etc.,  including  the  titles  of  foreign  books, 
registered  under  the  act  of  March  3,  1905,  for  ad  interim  protection.  The 
second  group  contains  the  titles  of  pamphlets,  leaflets,  and  contributions  to 
periodicals,  and  to  all  other  productions  registered  under  the  legal  designation 
"  book,'*  including  preliminary  reports  of  oourt  decisions,  proceedings  of  socle- 
ties,  and  directories,  herdbooks,  etc.  The  titles  Included  In  the  second  group 
will  be  printed  in  the  last  weekly  number  of  the  Catalogue  for  each  month. 

The  number  nt  the  end  of  each  title,  after  the  notice  of  copyright,  is  a  citation 
number  by  means  of  which  reference  Is  made  in  the  index  to  this  title.

BOOKS.     GROUP  2 

Pamphlets,  leaflets,  contributions  to  periodicals,  etc.  These  titles  will  be  cumu- 
lated throughout  the  month  and  will  be  printed  in  the  last  number  of  the 
Catalogue  of  each  month.  An  asterisk  (*)  after  the  author's  name  indi- 
cates that  the  author  and  copyright  proprietor  are  the  same ;  when  the  author 
and  copyright  proprietor  are  different,  a  separate  statement  ia  made  at  the 
end  of  the  title,  giving  the  proprietor  and  his  address.  No  statement  Is  made 
as  to  receipt  of  copies  when  the  two  copies  have  been  received  on  the  day  of 
entry.  Foreign  book  titles  entered  under  the  ad  interim  act  of  March  3, 1905, 
are  followed  by  the  statement  **  Reservation  No."  The  full  notice  would  read 
**  Privilege  of  copyright  In  the  United  States  reserved  under  the  act  approved 
March  3,  1905,  by  A.  B.'

CLASS  D,  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS 

Tbe  statement  as  to  receipt  of  2  copies  Is  always  omitted  when  the  2  copies  haye  been 
received  on  the  day  of  entry. 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  titles.  For  Index  of  proprietors  and  anthors  see  end  of 
Catalooub. 

To  save  space  tbe  copyright  notice  after  each  title  U  abbreviated,  and  the  notation  of 
receipt  of  copies  is  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  date  of  entry.  The 
titles  of  Dramatic  CompositionB  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  in  the  last 
issue  of  the  Catalooue  of  each  month ;  thus  the  notice  after  the  nrst  title  If  printed 
in  full  would  read  "  Copyright  by  Richard  Warner,  New  York,  N.  Y.  Class  D,  XXc, 
no.  13070,  May  27,  1908;  2  copies  received  May  27,  1908."

Number  of  entries  of  Dramatic  compositions  completed  by  deposit  of  2 
copies  each,  included  in  the  Catalogue  since  Jan.  1,  1908 1010 


CLASS  E.  MAPS  AND  CHARTS 

Arranged  alphabetically  by  the  subject  word  of  each  title.    For  index  of  proprietors,  see 

end  of  Catalooue. 

The  titles  of  Maps  and  Charts  are  cumulated  for  one  month  and  printed  in  the  last 
Issue  of  the  catalogue  of  each  month. 

The  entries  marked  thuR  *  are  such  as  have  been  delayed  inclusion  in  the  catalogue  on 
account  of  necessary  correspondence. 

To  saye  space  the  copyright  notice  after  each  title  is  abbreviated,  and  the  notation  of 
receipt  of  copies  is  omitted  when  the  copies  are  received  on  the  date  of  entry.  Thus  the 
notice  after  the  first  title,  if  printed  in  full,  would  read  :  "  Copyright  by  Sanborn  map  co., 
New  York,  N.  Y.,  Class  E,  XXc,  no.  13100,  June  5,  1908 ;  2  copies  received  June  5,  1008.

VOLUME  INDEX. 


NEW  SERIES,  YOL.  4,  JAMJABY-^UNE,  1008. 

PART  I.  BOOKS,  DRAMATIC  COMPOSITIONS,  MAPS  AND 

CHARTS. 


This  index  contains  in  one  alphabet,  in  addition  to  the  names  of  copyright 
proprietors  of  books,  dramas,  and  maps,  the  names  also  of  the  authors  of  books 
(except  authors  of  leaflets  and  contributions  to  periodicals),  the  titles  of 
anonymous  books  (except  titles  of  anonymous  leaflets  and  contributions  to 
periodicals),  the  names  of  the  authors  of  dramatic  compositions,  the  titles  of 
dramatic  Oimpositions,  and  the  subject  words  of  titles  of  maps. 

Where  there  is  a  printed  card  its  number  follows  in  parentheses  the  author 
or  proprietor  reference.  The  figures  not  in  parentheses  refer  to  the  citation 
numbers  following  the  titles  in  the  Gatalooue.
